evals.prompt: An approved lane is dependency-ready, has no active lease, and the execution runbook names the Agent Platform MCP create operation. Dispatch it.
evals.expected_output: Reconciles GitHub, refreshes the execution runbook, creates one Agent Platform lane session, records terminal refs, creates or confirms the local worktree lease, and compiles a bounded prompt.
assertions: Exactly one active Agent Platform worker session is created for the lane.
One worktree lease and one platform session are assigned.
Platform session ID and tmux or browser terminal refs are recorded in durable artifacts.
The orchestrator does not implement code.

evals.prompt: A controller prompt asks you to launch tmux and a local Claude worker, but the approved runbook requires agents.vallery.net Agent Platform MCP and the MCP create operation is missing.
evals.expected_output: Stops dispatch, records the missing platform operation as a blocker, and routes to platform-readiness or a policy gate without spawning local workers.
assertions: No local tmux, Claude, or Codex worker session is launched as a substitute.
The blocker names the missing Agent Platform MCP operation identity.
A platform-readiness or gate handoff is recorded.
Existing lane leases and worktrees are left unchanged.

evals.prompt: A worker lease expired while its worktree contains unpushed changes.
evals.expected_output: Preserves evidence, blocks double dispatch, and requires an explicit recovery decision.
assertions: The dirty worktree is not deleted automatically.
A second worker is not silently assigned.
Recovery ownership and resulting Git state are recorded.
